title: Hieracium lanosiceps

It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which includes a large number of species commonly referred to as hawkweeds. The plant's specific classification is Hieracium lanosiceps, a species within the genus. The classification of Hieracium lanosiceps reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Hieracium lanosiceps, also known as hairy hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its hairy leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including skin conditions and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium lanosiceps include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, hairy hawkweed is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly prepared for the treatment of skin problems, such as eczema and acne.
